concept: Winter / Season of Rest / Time of Dormancy
description: The coldest season of the year; time of dormancy and introspection. 'Shila' names the resting season—life withdrawn, world quiet, the earth sleeping.
sound_symbolism: The word settles into stillness. 'Shi' /ʃi/ begins with soft breath, cold arriving. Then 'la' rests quietly, winter settled.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'mua shila' (in winter). Fourth of the four seasons. Time of rest, reflection, and living on stored resources.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Winter models necessary dormancy—growth requires periods of rest, activity requires stillness.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Winter enforced rest—the season when outdoor work ceased and communities gathered inward.
